failure_surface: Algorithm incorrectly identified which rows to delete based on summing column values to zero; only 3 out of 4 rows were marked for deletion, leaving a single data row that should have been removed along with others.
root_cause: Greedy sequence detection algorithm incorrectly rejected valid row sequences when intermediate partial sums exceeded the floating-point tolerance threshold, even though the complete sequence summed to approximately zero.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: When searching for consecutive rows whose values sum to approximately zero, do not prematurely terminate a sequence based on intermediate partial sum thresholds; instead, continue accumulating until finding complete sequences where the total sum meets the criteria, then backtrack or use a different search strategy like sliding window over all possible start-end pairs.
evidence: Exploration showed all 4 data rows (2-5) form a sequence that sums to approximately zero, yet solution only deleted rows [3, 4, 5]
Row 2 with value 0.0100000000020373 remained in output despite being part of the zero-sum group
Algorithm breaks inner loop when abs(temp_sum) > amount_threshold during accumulation, causing early exit before checking if additional values would bring sum back toward zero
Final verification shows remaining row with non-zero Amount value indicates incomplete row deletion
Evaluation result shows test_case_results: [0], indicating complete task failure

memory_items.title: Intermediate Sum Thresholding Pitfall
memory_items.lesson: When identifying groups of consecutive values that sum to approximately zero, do not reject a candidate sequence solely because intermediate partial sums temporarily exceed your tolerance threshold—the final sum may still meet the criteria after additional terms are added.
memory_items.generalization: For cumulative aggregation tasks with tolerance checks, consider sliding-window or prefix-sum approaches that evaluate all possible contiguous subsequences rather than greedy forward-matching that terminates on intermediate threshold violations.

memory_items.title: Floating-Point Tolerance in Sequence Detection
memory_items.lesson: Floating-point precision errors require robust tolerance-based comparisons; always verify that entire candidate groups satisfy the condition before committing to selection or deletion actions.
memory_items.generalization: Apply absolute-value tolerance checks (e.g., abs(value) < epsilon) consistently across numeric comparison operations in spreadsheet row/column selection logic.

memory_items.title: Validate Complete Group Membership Before Modification
memory_items.lesson: Before deleting or modifying rows based on aggregated conditions, confirm all affected cells belong to complete qualifying groups by testing the full range first, then applying changes in bulk.
memory_items.generalization: Collect all target indices into a set before executing mutations that alter row positions; this prevents cascading index shifts and ensures consistent group-level operations.

edits.content:
## Floating-Point Group Selection

- **Partial Sums Warning**: When selecting rows whose column values sum to approximately zero, do not terminate sequence detection based on intermediate partial sums exceeding tolerance—complete sequences may still sum correctly after additional terms.
- **Search Strategy**: Prefer sliding-window or prefix-sum enumeration over all contiguous ranges rather than greedy accumulation that halts on threshold violations.
- **Verification Pattern**: Identify all candidate row groups before any deletions; apply modifications in reverse row order to prevent index shifting complications.
